name: Isaac Hayes
abstract: Isaac Lee Hayes Jr. (August 20, 1942 – August 10, 2008) was an American singer, songwriter, composer, and actor.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom McIntosh
about_quotes.evidence: They said, 'Well, let’s get somebody to supervise him.' Gordon says, 'Well, I know just the guy.' So, they asked me if I could come on in and supervise the music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom McIntosh — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: She says, “Well, in the singers you have Koko Taylor, Gary U.S. Bonds, Isaac Hayes and Lou Rawls... I was really glad to be a part of that.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Chico Hamilton) does not mention Isaac Hayes. The mention of Isaac Hayes is made by a third party (Mr. Kimery) in the context of discussing a manager who 'managed Isaac Hayes' and 'put the Staple singers, Isaac Hayes' in groups. The interviewee does not confirm a personal relationsh
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: We scoot over to Ardent Studios where Isaac Hayes — all these wonderful people have recorded down through the years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Henry Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing in an R&B band which was playing with Isaac Hayes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Henry Johnson interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995
